name: Open X-Embodiment
collection: Aggregated from many robotic learning datasets
embodiment: Single-arm, dual-arm, mobile manipulators and other robots
use: Cross-embodiment pretraining and action representation research
limitations: Highly heterogeneous action frequencies, cameras, robot morphologies and language styles.

name: AgiBot World / Colosseo
scale: >1M trajectories, 217 tasks, multiple real-world scenario categories
collection: Standardized real robot collection pipeline with human-in-the-loop verification
embodiment: Real robots in home, retail, office, restaurant and other settings
use: High-quality real-world pretraining source for WAMs such as DreamZero and related work
limitations: New ecosystem; long-term external validation and reproducibility are still developing.

name: DROID
collection: Distributed real-world collection with Franka robots and multi-camera setup
use: Commonly used in WAM/VLA pretraining and evaluation, including UWM and VLA-JEPA-like settings
limitations: Single dominant robot platform; limited coverage of dexterous hands and humanoids.

name: BridgeData V2
use: World-model pretraining, VLA training and latent-action research such as LAWM
limitations: Task and environment diversity is useful but narrower than OXE or AgiBot-scale corpora.

name: LIBERO
use: Common WAM/VLA benchmark for multitask and long-horizon evaluation
limitations: Simulation domain; physical realism and visual diversity are limited compared with real robots.

name: CALVIN
scale: Long-horizon language-conditioned manipulation dataset and benchmark
use: Evaluating long-horizon compositional manipulation and predictive policy representations
limitations: Embodiment and scene diversity remain relatively constrained.

name: RLBench
scale: 100 hand-designed tasks with scalable demonstration generation
collection: Simulation and motion planning generated demonstrations
use: Synthetic data generation, task coverage, ablation and world-model evaluation
limitations: Planner-generated behavior can differ from real human teleoperation and real robot dynamics.

name: OpenVid-1M
limitations: No robot actions, weak executability and large embodiment gap.

name: Ego-Exo4D
scale: Large-scale multi-view human skill video dataset
use: Human-to-robot transfer, first-person skill priors and latent action learning
limitations: Human embodiment differs from robot embodiment; retargeting and executability filtering are required.

name: UniTraj
collection: Trajectory datasets in driving and general motion forecasting contexts
embodiment: Vehicles / agents rather than robot manipulation
use: Reference for long-horizon trajectory representation and cross-domain temporal modeling
limitations: Not a standard robot manipulation dataset; relevance to WAM is indirect.
